# Authentication Overview

Authentication flow validates credentials against the identity store and issues a session token.
Every successful login records an audit event and includes the actor, scope, and timestamp.
If validation fails, the service records a denial event and returns an access failure.

Authentication step 1 keeps the audit trail consistent and explains why session revocation happens after suspicious activity.
Authentication step 2 keeps the audit trail consistent and explains why session revocation happens after suspicious activity.
Authentication step 3 keeps the audit trail consistent and explains why session revocation happens after suspicious activity.
Authentication step 4 keeps the audit trail consistent and explains why session revocation happens after suspicious activity.
Authentication step 5 keeps the audit trail consistent and explains why session revocation happens after suspicious activity.
